Hero.subtitle: Decide what your links do when someone visits them. No code.
Hero.note: No signup, free, ready in 30 seconds

Problems.title: The problem with
Problems.slide2Title: Your US affiliate offer gets clicked by someone in Germany.
Problems.slide3Title: A mobile user lands on your desktop version.
Problems.slide4Subtitle: Competitors, scrapers and the curious help themselves.

Steps.title: Three steps. Zero code.
Steps.step1Desc: No matter how long, ugly or complex. We turn it into a smart link.
Steps.step2Desc: Country, device, VPN, bots, schedule... Choose conditions, choose actions, combine them.
Steps.step3Desc: A single link that adapts to every visitor. You define the logic once. The link does the work always.
Steps.cta: Try it now · No account needed!

UseCases.title: For those who care where their clicks go
UseCases.persona1Desc: A single bio link that routes to YouTube, Spotify or Amazon depending on what her follower is looking for.
UseCases.persona2Desc: Runs campaigns across 5 channels. Knows exactly which ones convert and which are bots.
UseCases.persona3Desc: Mobile goes to her app. Desktop goes to the web. Not a single extra line of code.
UseCases.persona4Desc: Sends his portfolio to a client. Knows when they open it, from where, and how many times.
UseCases.persona4Tagline: Never ask «did you see it?» again.
UseCases.persona5Desc: Sets an expiry date on her launch offer. After that, the link redirects to the regular price.

RulesSection.title: If this happens, do that. For your links.
RulesSection.subtitle: Use 7 conditions and 4 actions to decide what happens when your link is visited. Without writing a single line of code.
RulesSection.hint: Tap each one to discover what it does
RulesSection.pillCountryDesc: Act based on the visitor's country
RulesSection.pillRedirectDesc: Send the user to a URL

Bento.title: One unique link, infinite ways to connect.

Pricing.title: Start free, scale whenever.
Pricing.freeDesc: Forever, seriously. No card, no account if you don't want one.

FAQ.title: Quick answers
FAQ.q1: How is this different from Bitly?
FAQ.a1: Bitly shortens URLs. Linkkk gives them behavior. You can define conditions (country, device, VPN, bots, schedule) and actions (redirect, block, password, webhook). A link that does different things depending on who clicks.
FAQ.q2: Do I need to know how to code?
FAQ.a2: No. The rules engine is visual: select conditions, choose actions, save. If you can fill out a form, you can use Linkkk.
FAQ.a3: Under 50 milliseconds. Your visitors won't notice any delay. Slow redirects kill conversions — ours don't.
FAQ.q4: What happens if I exceed my plan limits?
FAQ.a4: You can't create more links, but existing ones keep working. We never break active links. You can upgrade whenever you want.
FAQ.a5: The code is on GitHub. You can self-host it. And your destination URLs will always work independently — only redirects depend on us, and we've designed them so you can migrate if needed.
FAQ.a6: Passwords hashed with bcrypt. Data encrypted. IPs anonymized for GDPR compliance. We don't sell data to anyone. Period.
FAQ.q7: Can I try everything before paying?
FAQ.a7: Yes. The free plan includes rules, analytics and QR codes. PRO just removes limits. You can even try as a guest without giving your email.

gettingStarted.title: Getting started
gettingStarted.subtitle: Linkkk is not a URL shortener. It's a rule engine for your links. Decide what happens when someone clicks: redirect, block, ask for a password or fire a webhook, all based on conditions like country, device or VPN.
gettingStarted.differenceTitle: How is it different from Bitly?
gettingStarted.differenceDesc: Bitly shortens URLs. Linkkk gives them behavior. You can define conditions (country, device, VPN, bots, schedule) and actions (redirect, block, password, webhook). A link that does different things depending on who clicks.
gettingStarted.guestDesc: No sign-up, no email. Create up to 3 links that last 7 days. Includes 1 rule per link with 1 condition. Perfect for testing.
gettingStarted.registeredDesc: Sign up with email or Google/GitHub. Free plan with 50 permanent links, 3 rules per link, full analytics and customizable QR codes.
gettingStarted.stepsTitle: Create your first link in 30 seconds
gettingStarted.step1Desc: No matter how long or ugly it is. Press the + button or "Create link".
gettingStarted.step2Desc: Add conditions and actions. For example: "If the visitor uses VPN → block".
gettingStarted.step3Desc: The link adapts to each visitor automatically. You define the logic once.
gettingStarted.navNextDesc: Create, edit, delete and customize your links

links.title: Links
links.subtitle: Every link in Linkkk is a smart link. Beyond shortening URLs, you can customize it, add rules and track who visits it.
links.createDesc: Press the + button in the navigation or "Create link" in the dashboard. You only need the destination URL. Linkkk automatically generates a short, unique slug.
links.createFieldDest: Destination URL: The URL your link will redirect to (required)
links.createFieldSuffix: Custom suffix: Instead of a random slug, choose your own (registered users only)
links.suffixDesc: If you have an account, you can choose a custom suffix for your link. For example: linkkk.dev/r/my-offer
links.suffixNote: Note: Suffixes must be unique. If it already exists, you'll need to choose another.
links.stateActiveDesc: The link works normally. Evaluates rules and redirects.
links.stateInactiveDesc: The link shows a "link disabled" page. You can reactivate it whenever you want.
links.stateExpiredDesc: Guests only. After 7 days from creation, the link is deleted and will not work.
links.editDesc: From the dashboard, click on any link to open its editing panel. You can change the destination URL, suffix, rules and status. To delete a link, use the option in the link menu. This action is irreversible.
links.organizeDesc: Registered users can organize links with groups and tags. Both are managed from the dashboard using the Organize button.
links.groupsDesc: A group is a folder-like container. Each link can belong to one group. Groups have a name and a color. Use them to separate campaigns, clients or projects — for example "Black Friday", "Client A" or "Social".
links.tagsDesc: Tags are labels you can attach to multiple links at once. A link can have several tags. Use them for cross-cutting categories like "paid", "email" or "landing". Tags also have a name and a color.

rules.title: Rules
rules.subtitle: Rules are the heart of Linkkk. With them you define what happens when someone clicks on your link, based on who they are, where they're accessing from and when they do it.
rules.howDesc: Each rule has three parts: conditions (if this happens), actions (do that) and an optional alternative action (if not met, do this instead).
rules.conditionCountryDesc: Filters by the visitor's geographic location. Uses ISO codes (ES, US, DE...).
rules.conditionDeviceDesc: Distinguishes between mobile, tablet and desktop.
rules.conditionVpnDesc: Detects if the visitor is using a VPN or proxy.
rules.conditionBotDesc: Identifies bots, crawlers and scrapers automatically.
rules.conditionDateDesc: Activates rules before or after a specific date.
rules.conditionCountDesc: Acts based on how many times the link has been visited.
rules.conditionCountOperators: equal to, greater than, less than
rules.logicDesc: When a rule has multiple conditions, you can choose how to combine them:
rules.logicAndDesc: All conditions must be met. More restrictive.
rules.logicOrDesc: Just one condition needs to be met. More flexible.
rules.actionRedirectDesc: Sends the visitor to a different URL. The most common action. Ideal for geo-segmentation, separating mobile/desktop or A/B testing.
rules.actionBlockDesc: Shows an access blocked page. Useful for filtering bots, VPNs or unwanted countries.
rules.actionPasswordDesc: The visitor must enter a password to access the destination. The password is stored hashed with bcrypt.
rules.actionWebhookDesc: Fires an HTTP request to the URL you configure. Perfect for integrations, notifications or automations.
rules.priorityDesc: When a link has multiple rules, they are evaluated by priority (from lowest to highest number). The first rule whose conditions are met is executed. If no rule matches, the link redirects to the original destination URL.

analytics.title: Analytics
analytics.subtitle: Every click on your links is recorded. Linkkk shows you who accesses, from where, with what device and whether it's a real visitor or a bot.
analytics.trackVpnDesc: If the visitor uses a VPN or proxy
analytics.trackBotDesc: If it's a crawler or scraper
analytics.aggregatedDesc: In addition to individual history, Linkkk calculates aggregate metrics for each link:
analytics.aggregatedDirectVsQrDesc: Ratio of direct clicks vs QR scans
analytics.aggregatedVpnBotsDesc: Number of accesses from VPN and detected bots
analytics.dashboardDesc: In the dashboard you'll see a global summary with:
analytics.privacyDesc: IP addresses are anonymized to comply with GDPR. We do not store personally identifiable data from visitors. We only record aggregated data necessary for analytics and rule evaluation.

qrCodes.title: QR Codes
qrCodes.subtitle: Every Linkkk link has its own customizable QR code. Best part: QR scans are tracked separately, so you can measure offline vs online.
qrCodes.customDesc: From the link editing panel, go to the QR tab to customize:
qrCodes.customFgColorDesc: The color of the QR dots. Default black.
qrCodes.customLogoDesc: Upload your logo and it will appear centered in the QR. Adjust the size to your liking.
qrCodes.customDotStyleDesc: Choose between squares, rounded or circular dots.
qrCodes.customCornerStyleDesc: QR corners can be square or rounded.
qrCodes.trackingDesc: When someone scans the QR, Linkkk records it as an access with source "QR" instead of "direct". In your analytics you'll see the ratio of direct clicks vs scans, useful for measuring physical campaigns (flyers, cards, posters).
qrCodes.dynamicDesc: Linkkk QR codes are dynamic: they point to your short link, not the destination URL. This means you can change the destination without reprinting the QR. Rules also apply to QR scans.

plans.title: Plans
plans.subtitle: Linkkk has a freemium model. You can use the platform without paying anything, even without creating an account. PRO just removes limits.
plans.faq1Q: What happens if I exceed the limits?
plans.faq1A: You get a warning. Existing links keep working, we never break active links. You simply won't be able to create new ones until you upgrade.
plans.faq2Q: Can I cancel PRO at any time?
plans.faq2A: Yes. When you cancel, you keep PRO until the end of the paid period. After that you switch to Standard automatically. Your links remain.
plans.faq3Q: Can I go from guest to Standard?
plans.faq3A: Yes, you just need to register. Your guest links are migrated to your new account and no longer have an expiration date.
plans.faq4Q: What payment methods do you accept?
plans.faq4A: Payment is processed through Stripe. We accept credit/debit cards and other methods depending on your country. Linkkk never stores your card data.

security.title: Security
security.subtitle: Security is not an add-on in Linkkk, it's part of the design. From data encryption to bot detection, every layer is designed to protect both you and the visitors of your links.
security.dataPasswordDesc: Hashed with bcrypt (12 rounds, OWASP standard). Never stored in plain text. This applies to both user passwords and protected link passwords.
security.dataIpDesc: IP addresses are used internally for geolocation, VPN detection and IP condition rule evaluation. In the analytics panel they are always shown anonymized (last octet masked). Access data is fully anonymized after 12 months.
security.dataPaymentDesc: Payments are processed entirely through Stripe (PCI DSS Level 1). Linkkk does not store card numbers or banking data.
security.dataNoSellDesc: Analytics data is yours. We don't share or sell it to third parties. Period.
security.authJwtDesc: Sessions use JWT tokens with 30-day expiration, stored in HttpOnly + SameSite cookies to prevent XSS and CSRF.
security.authOauthDesc: You can sign up with Google or GitHub. We use the standard OAuth 2.0 authorization flow, without accessing unnecessary data from your account.
security.authCsrfDesc: All operations that modify data require a valid CSRF token, preventing request forgery attacks.
security.detectionBotDesc: Linkkk automatically identifies crawlers, scrapers and bots through User-Agent analysis. You can use it as a condition in your rules to block or redirect them.
security.detectionVpnDesc: We detect connections through VPNs and proxies using IP intelligence. Useful for filtering non-genuine traffic or limiting access by real region.
security.infraHeadersDesc: Helmet.js configures CSP, HSTS, X-Frame-Options and other security headers on all server responses.
security.infraRateLimitDesc: Sensitive endpoints (login, password verification) have request limits to prevent brute force attacks.
security.infraCorsDesc: Only authorized domains can make requests to the API. Access from non-permitted domains is blocked.
security.openSourceDesc: Linkkk's source code is available on GitHub. You can audit security, report vulnerabilities or contribute. If Linkkk closes, you can self-host it.
security.gdprDesc: As a Linkkk user, you have the right to:
security.gdprAccessDesc: Check what data we have about you
security.gdprDeletionDesc: Delete your account and all your data from Settings
